active_projects.completion_summary: Documented D-parametric architecture as primary representation theorem path. Base TM uses D=Int parametrically (no characterization theorem exists), dense TM can use D-from-syntax via Cantor as auxiliary. Updated ROAD_MAP.md strategy and 3 Lean files with architectural documentation.

artifacts.summary: Primary literature approaches: Henkin-style completeness, Prior/Burgess/Goldblatt, D-from-syntax vs D-parametric. Conclusion: D-parametric is correct primary architecture.
Alternative approaches: Montanari & de Rijke 1995 two-sorted MTL precedent, BAO/Stone duality limitations, category-theoretic and Since/Until approaches. Confirms D-parametric recommendation.
Synthesis of both teammates: unanimous recommendation for D-parametric with axioms. D-from-syntax infeasible for base logic, D-from-syntax kept as auxiliary for dense/discrete specialization.

active_projects.description: Prove dense algebraic completeness using D = Rat. Requires: (1) a sorry-free BFMCS construction over Rat (adapting the Int construction with density-exploiting witness placement), (2) proving the DN axiom is valid in DenseCanonicalTaskFrame Rat (truth lemma shows density axiom holds because Rat is densely ordered), (3) wiring dense_representation_conditional to obtain valid_dense φ → ⊢_dense φ. Does not overlap with task 982 (TimelineQuot approach).

artifacts.summary: Analyzed D-parametric infrastructure for dense algebraic completeness with D=Rat. Core gap: construct_bfmcs function. Recommended: embed CanonicalMCS into Rat via Cantor theorem.

active_projects.description: Prove discrete algebraic completeness using D = Int. Requires: (1) proving DF and DP axioms are valid in DiscreteCanonicalTaskFrame Int (the G-operator Stone relation on Int-indexed MCS families satisfies immediate successor conditions), (2) using the BFMCS construction from task 986 for the discrete proof system, (3) wiring discrete_representation_conditional to obtain valid_discrete φ → ⊢_discrete φ. Does not overlap with task 981 (removes axiom from staged construction; this uses algebraic D = Int approach).
